---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Bayowa"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)

# 1. Read and Initial Clean
blackpop_raw <- read_csv("Data_raw/nhgis0003_ts_nominal_tract.csv") %>%
  clean_names()

# 2. Filter to DC (Using name and potential state code if needed)
blackpop_dc <- blackpop_raw %>%
  filter(state == "District of Columbia")

# 3. Select and Transform to Long Format
dc_black_long <- blackpop_dc %>%
  select(
    nhgiscode,
    tracta,
    starts_with("name"),
    starts_with("b18ab")
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("b18ab"),
    names_to = "year_raw",
    values_to = "black_population"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    year = as.integer(str_extract(year_raw, "\\d{4}")),
    # Dynamically grab the correct tract name based on the year
    tract_name = case_when(
      year == 1970 ~ name1970,
      year == 1980 ~ name1980,
      year == 1990 ~ name1990,
      year == 2000 ~ name2000,
      year == 2010 ~ name2010,
      year == 2020 ~ name2020,
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

# 4. Final Cleaning and Export Prep
dc_black_population_clean <- dc_black_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(black_population)) %>%
  select(
    nhgiscode,
    tracta,
    tract_name,
    year,
    black_population
  ) %>%
  arrange(year, tracta)

# Create directory if it doesn't exist before saving
if(!dir.exists("Cleaned_data_EDJones")) dir.create("Cleaned_data_EDJones")

write_csv(dc_black_population_clean, "Cleaned_data_EDJones/dc_black_population_tract_1970_2020.csv")

# View result
View(dc_black_population_clean)

```


```{r}
# 1. Read 1960 Data
race_1960_raw <- read_csv("Data_raw/nhgis0002_ds92_1960_tract.csv") %>%
  clean_names()

# 2. Filter and Calculate Percentages
race_1960_dc <- race_1960_raw %>%
  # FIPS 11 is DC; also checking 'state' column for safety if available
  filter(statea == "11" | state == "District of Columbia") %>% 
  mutate(
    nhgiscode = gisjoin,
    year = 1960L,
    # Ensure tracta is a 4-digit string with leading zeros
    tracta_clean = str_pad(as.character(as.integer(tracta)), width = 4, pad = "0"),
    total_population = b9t001,
    black_population = b7b003, # Non-white proxy
    black_percent = black_population / total_population,
    tract_name = areaname
  ) %>%
  select(
    nhgiscode, 
    tracta = tracta_clean, 
    tract_name, 
    year, 
    black_population,
    total_population, 
    black_percent
  )

# View result
View(race_1960_dc)
```


```{r}

race_raw <- read_csv("Data_raw/nhgis0003_ts_nominal_tract.csv") %>%
  clean_names()

race_dc <- race_raw %>%
  filter(state == "District of Columbia")

# Optimized selection using starts_with to keep it clean
race_dc_selected <- race_dc %>%
  select(
    nhgiscode,
    tracta,
    starts_with("name"),
    starts_with("b18aa"), # White
    starts_with("b18ab"), # Black
    starts_with("b18ac"), # AI/AN
    starts_with("b18ad"), # Asian / PI / Other
    starts_with("b18ae")  # Two or more
  )


```



```{r}
#Pivot to long format
race_long <- race_dc_selected %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("b18"),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "population"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    year = as.integer(str_extract(variable, "\\d{4}")),
    race = case_when(
      str_detect(variable, "b18aa") ~ "White",
      str_detect(variable, "b18ab") ~ "Black",
      str_detect(variable, "b18ac") ~ "American Indian or Alaska Native",
      str_detect(variable, "b18ad") ~ "Asian / Pacific Islander / Other",
      str_detect(variable, "b18ae") ~ "Two or More Races"
    )
  )

# Total population per tract per year
dc_total_population_clean <- race_long %>%
  group_by(nhgiscode, tracta, year) %>%
  summarise(
    total_population = sum(population, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Specific Black population subset with dynamic name attachment
dc_black_population_clean <- race_long %>%
  filter(race == "Black") %>%
  mutate(
    tract_name = case_when(
      year == 1970 ~ name1970,
      year == 1980 ~ name1980,
      year == 1990 ~ name1990,
      year == 2000 ~ name2000,
      year == 2010 ~ name2010,
      year == 2020 ~ name2020,
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  select(
    nhgiscode,
    tracta,
    tract_name,
    year,
    black_population = population
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(black_population))
```


```{r}
dc_black_percent <- dc_black_population_clean %>%
  left_join(
    dc_total_population_clean,
    by = c("nhgiscode", "tracta", "year")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Handle potential division by zero just in case
    black_percent = if_else(total_population > 0, black_population / total_population, 0)
  )

# Output check
if(!dir.exists("Cleaned_data_EDJones")) dir.create("Cleaned_data_EDJones")
write_csv(dc_black_percent, "Cleaned_data_EDJones/dc_black_population_percentage_1970_2020.csv")

View(dc_black_percent)

```


```{r}
# Bind the 1960 proxy data with the 1970-2020 data
dc_black_percent_full <- bind_rows(
  race_1960_dc,
  dc_black_percent        
) %>%
  arrange(nhgiscode, year)

write_csv(dc_black_percent_full, "Cleaned_data_EDJones/dc_black_population_percentage_1960_2020.csv")

# Final verification
glimpse(dc_black_percent_full)
```


```{r}
dc_black_change <- dc_black_percent_full %>%
  arrange(nhgiscode, year) %>%
  group_by(nhgiscode) %>%
  mutate(
    black_pop_change = black_population - lag(black_population),
    # Use if_else to prevent division by zero or NA
    black_pop_pct_change = if_else(
      condition = lag(black_population) > 0, 
      true = (black_pop_change / lag(black_population)) * 100, 
      false = NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Exporting the change data
write_csv(dc_black_change, "Cleaned_data_EDJones/dc_black_population_change_1960_2020.csv")

View(dc_black_change)
```


```{r}
dc_black_segregation <- dc_black_percent_full %>%
  mutate(
    segregation_category = case_when(
      black_percent < 0.25 ~ "0–25%",
      black_percent >= 0.25 & black_percent < 0.50 ~ "25–50%",
      black_percent >= 0.50 & black_percent < 0.75 ~ "50–75%",
      black_percent >= 0.75 ~ "75–100%",
      TRUE ~ "Unknown"
    ),
    # Converting to factor so it displays in order on charts/legends
    segregation_category = factor(
      segregation_category, 
      levels = c("0–25%", "25–50%", "50–75%", "75–100%")
    )
  )

# Exporting the segregation data
write_csv(dc_black_segregation, "Cleaned_data_EDJones/dc_black_segregation_categories.csv")

# Quick check on category counts
table(dc_black_segregation$segregation_category, useNA = "always")
View(dc_black_segregation)
```

### Demographics- age, sex
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(jsonlite)

output_dir <- "Cleaned_data_EDJones"
if(!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)

#Process 1960 Total Population (B8D) 
age_sex_1960_raw <- read_csv("Data_raw/nhgis0015_ds92_Age_sex_1960_tract.csv") %>%
  clean_names()

dc_age_sex_1960_clean <- age_sex_1960_raw %>%
  filter(statea == "11") %>%
  select(gisjoin, tracta, areaname, b8d001:b8d050) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = b8d001:b8d050, names_to = "variable", values_to = "count") %>%
  mutate(
    # Refined sex logic: extract digits and check if odd (male) or even (female)
    var_num = as.numeric(str_extract(variable, "\\d+")),
    sex = if_else(var_num %% 2 == 1, "male", "female"),
    age_detailed = case_when(
      var_num %in% 1:2 ~ "Under 1 year",
      var_num %in% 3:4 ~ "1 year",
      var_num %in% 5:6 ~ "2 years",
      var_num %in% 7:8 ~ "3 years",
      var_num %in% 9:10 ~ "4 years",
      var_num %in% 11:12 ~ "5 years",
      var_num %in% 13:14 ~ "6 years",
      var_num %in% 15:16 ~ "7 years",
      var_num %in% 17:18 ~ "8 years",
      var_num %in% 19:20 ~ "9 years",
      var_num %in% 21:22 ~ "10 years",
      var_num %in% 23:24 ~ "11 years",
      var_num %in% 25:26 ~ "12 years",
      var_num %in% 27:28 ~ "13 years",
      var_num %in% 29:30 ~ "14 years",
      var_num %in% 31:32 ~ "15 years",
      var_num %in% 33:34 ~ "16 years",
      var_num %in% 35:36 ~ "17 years",
      var_num %in% 37:38 ~ "18 years",
      var_num %in% 39:40 ~ "19 years",
      var_num %in% 41:42 ~ "20 years",
      var_num %in% 43:44 ~ "21-24 years",
      var_num %in% 45:46 ~ "75-79 years",
      var_num %in% 47:48 ~ "80-84 years",
      var_num %in% 49:50 ~ "85 years and over",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    age_group = case_when(
      age_detailed %in% c("Under 1 year", "1 year", "2 years", "3 years", "4 years") ~ "Under 5 years",
      age_detailed %in% c("5 years", "6 years", "7 years", "8 years", "9 years") ~ "5 to 9 years",
      age_detailed %in% c("10 years", "11 years", "12 years", "13 years", "14 years") ~ "10 to 14 years",
      age_detailed %in% c("15 years", "16 years", "17 years") ~ "15 to 17 years",
      age_detailed %in% c("18 years", "19 years") ~ "18 and 19 years",
      age_detailed == "20 years" ~ "20 years",
      age_detailed == "21-24 years" ~ "22 to 24 years",
      age_detailed %in% c("75-79 years", "80-84 years") ~ "75 to 84 years",
      age_detailed == "85 years and over" ~ "85 years and over",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    year = 1960L,
    population = "total",
    source = "1960 Census - NT18/B8D"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(age_group), count >= 0) %>%
  group_by(gisjoin, tracta, areaname, year, population, sex, age_group, source) %>%
  summarise(count = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  # Standardize column names for binding later
  rename(geographic_id = gisjoin, tract_id = tracta, location_name = areaname) %>%
  mutate(tract_id = as.character(tract_id))

# Process 1970-2020 Total Population (B58)
b58_raw <- read_csv("Data_raw/nhgis0014_ts_nominal_Age_sex_1970_2020_tract.csv") %>%
  clean_names()

dc_b58 <- b58_raw %>%
  filter(statefp %in% c(11, "11") | state == "District of Columbia")

# Extract metadata for names
b58_tract_names <- dc_b58 %>%
  select(nhgiscode, tracta, starts_with("name")) %>%
  distinct()

dc_b58_long <- dc_b58 %>%
  select(nhgiscode, tracta, b58aa1970:b58bj2020) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = b58aa1970:b58bj2020, names_to = "variable", values_to = "count") %>%
  mutate(
    sex = if_else(str_detect(variable, "b58a[a-r]"), "male", "female"),
    age_code = str_extract(variable, "(?<=b58)[a-z]{2}"),
    year = as.integer(str_extract(variable, "\\d{4}")),
    age_group = case_when(
      age_code %in% c("aa", "as") ~ "Under 5 years",
      age_code %in% c("ab", "at") ~ "5 to 9 years",
      age_code %in% c("ac", "au") ~ "10 to 14 years",
      age_code %in% c("ad", "av") ~ "15 to 17 years",
      age_code %in% c("ae", "aw") ~ "18 and 19 years",
      age_code %in% c("af", "ax") ~ "20 years",
      age_code %in% c("ag", "ay") ~ "21 years",
      age_code %in% c("ah", "az") ~ "22 to 24 years",
      age_code %in% c("ai", "ba") ~ "25 to 29 years",
      age_code %in% c("aj", "bb") ~ "30 to 34 years",
      age_code %in% c("ak", "bc") ~ "35 to 44 years",
      age_code %in% c("al", "bd") ~ "45 to 54 years",
      age_code %in% c("am", "be") ~ "55 to 59 years",
      age_code %in% c("an", "bf") ~ "60 and 61 years",
      age_code %in% c("ao", "bg") ~ "62 to 64 years",
      age_code %in% c("ap", "bh") ~ "65 to 74 years",
      age_code %in% c("aq", "bi") ~ "75 to 84 years",
      age_code %in% c("ar", "bj") ~ "85 years and over",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  left_join(b58_tract_names, by = c("nhgiscode", "tracta")) %>%
  mutate(
    location_name = case_when(
      year == 1970 ~ name1970,
      year == 1980 ~ name1980,
      year == 1990 ~ name1990,
      year == 2000 ~ name2000,
      year == 2010 ~ name2010,
      year == 2020 ~ name2020,
      TRUE ~ paste("Tract", tracta)
    ),
    population = "total",
    source = "NHGIS Time Series B58"
  ) %>%
  select(geographic_id = nhgiscode, tract_id = tracta, location_name, year, population, sex, age_group, count, source) %>%
  mutate(tract_id = as.character(tract_id))

# Combine and Finalize 
dc_age_sex_total_all <- bind_rows(dc_age_sex_1960_clean, dc_b58_long) %>%
  mutate(
    decade = paste0(floor(year / 10) * 10, "s"),
    data_quality = if_else(year == 1960, "limited - missing ages 25-64", "complete - B58 standardized")
  ) %>%
  arrange(year, tract_id, sex, age_group)

# Export & Summarize
write_csv(dc_age_sex_total_all, file.path(output_dir, "dc_age_sex_total_1960_2020.csv"))

total_sex_summary <- dc_age_sex_total_all %>%
  group_by(year, sex) %>%
  summarise(total_pop = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = sex, values_from = total_pop)

print(total_sex_summary)
View(dc_age_sex_total_all)
```

### Income- Household income(NHGIS)
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)


# Create output directory
output_dir <- "Cleaned_data_EDJones"
if(!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)

cat("Starting DC Income Data Processing (1960-2020)...\n")
cat("Output directory:", output_dir, "\n")


#PROCESS 1960 FAMILY INCOME DATA (Census)

cat("\nProcessing 1960 family income data...\n")

income_1960_raw <- read_csv("Data_raw/nhgis0008_ds92_1960HouseholdIncome_tract.csv") %>%
  clean_names()

# Filter for DC (STATEA = 11 for DC)
dc_income_1960 <- income_1960_raw %>%
  filter(statea == "11")

# Clean and prepare 1960 FAMILY income data
dc_income_1960_clean <- dc_income_1960 %>%
  select(
    gisjoin,
    year,
    tracta,
    areaname,
    # FAMILY income columns for 1959 (B8W table)
    b8w001:b8w013
  ) %>%
  # Convert to long format
  pivot_longer(
    cols = b8w001:b8w013,
    names_to = "variable_code",
    values_to = "families"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Create meaningful income categories
    income_category = case_when(
      variable_code == "b8w001" ~ "<$1,000",
      variable_code == "b8w002" ~ "$1,000–1,999",
      variable_code == "b8w003" ~ "$2,000–2,999",
      variable_code == "b8w004" ~ "$3,000–3,999",
      variable_code == "b8w005" ~ "$4,000–4,999",
      variable_code == "b8w006" ~ "$5,000–5,999",
      variable_code == "b8w007" ~ "$6,000–6,999",
      variable_code == "b8w008" ~ "$7,000–7,999",
      variable_code == "b8w009" ~ "$8,000–8,999",
      variable_code == "b8w010" ~ "$9,000–9,999",
      variable_code == "b8w011" ~ "$10,000–14,999",
      variable_code == "b8w012" ~ "$15,000–24,999",
      variable_code == "b8w013" ~ "$25,000+"
    ),
    # Remove original variable code
    variable_code = NULL
  ) %>%
  # Add metadata
  mutate(
    unit = "families",  # Important: this is FAMILY income
    income_year = 1959,  # Income reported is for 1959
    data_type = "family_income",
    source = "1960 Census - Families by Income in 1959 (B8W table)",
    note = "1960 data reports FAMILY income, not household income. Filename is mislabeled."
  ) %>%
  rename(count = families)  # Standardize column name

cat("✅ 1960 data processed:", nrow(dc_income_1960_clean), "observations\n")


#PROCESS 1970-2000 HOUSEHOLD INCOME DATA (NHGIS Time Series)


cat("\nProcessing 1970-2000 household income data...\n")

income_raw <- read_csv(
  "Data_raw/nhgis0005_ts_houseincome_nominal_tract.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  clean_names()

income_dc <- income_raw %>%
  filter(state == "District of Columbia")

# Process 1970-2000 household income
dc_income_1970_2000 <- income_dc %>%
  select(
    nhgiscode,
    tracta,
    name1970, name1980, name1990, name2000,
    # Household income categories (4 brackets)
    bs7aa1970, bs7aa1980, bs7aa1990, bs7aa2000, # <$10k
    bs7ab1970, bs7ab1980, bs7ab1990, bs7ab2000, # $10k–14,999
    bs7ac1970, bs7ac1980, bs7ac1990, bs7ac2000, # $15k–24,999
    bs7ad1970, bs7ad1980, bs7ad1990, bs7ad2000  # $25k+
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("bs7"),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "households"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    year = str_extract(variable, "\\d{4}") %>% as.integer(),
    income_category = case_when(
      str_detect(variable, "bs7aa") ~ "<$10,000",
      str_detect(variable, "bs7ab") ~ "$10,000–14,999",
      str_detect(variable, "bs7ac") ~ "$15,000–24,999",
      str_detect(variable, "bs7ad") ~ "$25,000+"
    ),
    tract_name = case_when(
      year == 1970 ~ name1970,
      year == 1980 ~ name1980,
      year == 1990 ~ name1990,
      year == 2000 ~ name2000
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(households)) %>%
  select(
    nhgiscode,
    tracta,
    tract_name,
    year,
    income_category,
    households
  ) %>%
  # Add metadata
  mutate(
    unit = "households",
    income_year = year,
    data_type = "household_income",
    source = "NHGIS Time Series - Household Income (BS7 table)",
    note = "Nominal household income data. 4 income brackets."
  ) %>%
  rename(count = households)  # Standardize column name

cat("✅ 1970-2000 data processed:", nrow(dc_income_1970_2000), "observations\n")
cat("   Years:", paste(sort(unique(dc_income_1970_2000$year)), collapse = ", "), "\n")



# Read 2010 ACS data
acs_2010_raw <- read_csv("Data_raw/nhgis0009_ds191Household_MedianHouseincome_2008_20125_tract.csv") %>%
  clean_names()

# Filter for DC
dc_acs_2010 <- acs_2010_raw %>%
  filter(statea == "11")

# Process 2010 income distribution (QU0 = B19001 household income table)
dc_income_2010 <- dc_acs_2010 %>%
  select(
    gisjoin,
    tracta,
    name_e,
    # 16 income brackets from ACS table B19001
    qu0e002:qu0e017
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = qu0e002:qu0e017,
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "count"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Map 16 ACS brackets to 4 categories for consistency
    income_category = case_when(
      variable == "qu0e002" ~ "<$10,000",
      variable == "qu0e003" ~ "$10,000–14,999",
      variable %in% c("qu0e004", "qu0e005") ~ "$15,000–24,999",
      TRUE ~ "$25,000+"  # Combine all higher brackets
    ),
    year = 2010,
    tract_name = name_e,
    unit = "households",
    income_year = 2012,  # ACS reports in 2012 inflation-adjusted dollars
    data_type = "household_income",
    source = "ACS 2008-2012 - Household Income (B19001 table)",
    note = "2012 inflation-adjusted dollars. 16 ACS brackets mapped to 4."
  ) %>%
  # Sum counts for each category (since we combined brackets)
  group_by(gisjoin, tracta, tract_name, year, income_category, 
           unit, income_year, data_type, source, note) %>%
  summarise(count = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

cat("✅ 2010 data processed:", nrow(dc_income_2010), "observations\n")



# Read 2020 ACS data
acs_2020_raw <- read_csv("Data_raw/nhgis0009_ds262_Household_MedianHouseincome_2018_20225_tract.csv") %>%
  clean_names()

# Filter for DC
dc_acs_2020 <- acs_2020_raw %>%
  filter(statea == "11")

# Process 2020 income distribution (AQP5 = B19001 household income table)
dc_income_2020 <- dc_acs_2020 %>%
  select(
    gisjoin,
    tracta,
    name_e,
    # 16 income brackets from ACS table B19001
    aqp5e002:aqp5e017
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = aqp5e002:aqp5e017,
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "count"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Map 16 ACS brackets to 4 categories for consistency
    income_category = case_when(
      variable == "aqp5e002" ~ "<$10,000",
      variable == "aqp5e003" ~ "$10,000–14,999",
      variable %in% c("aqp5e004", "aqp5e005") ~ "$15,000–24,999",
      TRUE ~ "$25,000+"  # Combine all higher brackets
    ),
    year = 2020,
    tract_name = name_e,
    unit = "households",
    income_year = 2022,  # ACS reports in 2022 inflation-adjusted dollars
    data_type = "household_income",
    source = "ACS 2018-2022 - Household Income (B19001 table)",
    note = "2022 inflation-adjusted dollars. 16 ACS brackets mapped to 4."
  ) %>%
  # Sum counts for each category (since we combined brackets)
  group_by(gisjoin, tracta, tract_name, year, income_category,
           unit, income_year, data_type, source, note) %>%
  summarise(count = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

cat("✅ 2020 data processed:", nrow(dc_income_2020), "observations\n")



cat("\nStandardizing all datasets to common format...\n")

# Function to standardize column names and structure
standardize_income_dataset <- function(data) {
  cols <- names(data)
  
  # 1. Manually assign Geographic ID based on what exists
  if ("gisjoin" %in% cols) {
    data$geographic_id <- as.character(data$gisjoin)
  } else if ("nhgiscode" %in% cols) {
    data$geographic_id <- as.character(data$nhgiscode)
  } else {
    data$geographic_id <- NA_character_
  }
  
  # 2. Manually assign Location Name based on what exists
  if ("tract_name" %in% cols) {
    data$location_name <- as.character(data$tract_name)
  } else if ("areaname" %in% cols) {
    data$location_name <- as.character(data$areaname)
  } else if ("name_e" %in% cols) {
    data$location_name <- as.character(data$name_e)
  } else {
    data$location_name <- paste("Tract", data$tracta)
  }

  # 3. Now run the pipe for the logic that is consistent across all years
  data %>%
    mutate(
      tract_id = as.character(tracta),
      # Mapping to the 4-category broad schema for your longitudinal analysis
      income_category_broad = case_when(
        income_category %in% c("<$1,000", "$1,000–1,999", "$2,000–2,999",
                               "$3,000–3,999", "$4,000–4,999", "$5,000–5,999",
                               "$6,000–6,999", "$7,000–7,999", "$8,000–8,999",
                               "$9,000–9,999", "<$10,000") ~ "<$10,000",
        income_category == "$10,000–14,999" ~ "$10,000–14,999",
        income_category == "$15,000–24,999" ~ "$15,000–24,999",
        TRUE ~ "$25,000+"
      )
    ) %>%
    select(
      geographic_id,
      tract_id,
      location_name,
      year,
      income_category,
      income_category_broad,
      count,
      unit,
      income_year,
      data_type,
      source,
      note
    )
}

# Apply standardization
dc_income_1960_std <- standardize_income_dataset(dc_income_1960_clean)
dc_income_1970_2000_std <- standardize_income_dataset(dc_income_1970_2000)
dc_income_2010_std <- standardize_income_dataset(dc_income_2010)
dc_income_2020_std <- standardize_income_dataset(dc_income_2020)

# Combine All Decades
cat("\nCombining all decades (1960-2020)...\n")

dc_income_all_years <- bind_rows(
  dc_income_1960_std,
  dc_income_1970_2000_std,
  dc_income_2010_std,
  dc_income_2020_std
) %>%
  arrange(year, tract_id) %>%
  mutate(
    decade = paste0(floor(year / 10), "0s"),
    comparison_validity = case_when(
      year == 1960 & unit == "families" ~ "limited: family vs household",
      year >= 1970 & unit == "households" ~ "good: consistent unit",
      TRUE ~ "check unit consistency"
    )
  )

cat("✅ Combined dataset created:", nrow(dc_income_all_years), "total observations\n")

# ==============================================================================
# 8. ADD INFLATION ADJUSTMENT FOR COMPARABILITY
# ==============================================================================

cat("\nAdding inflation adjustment for cross-decade comparison...\n")

# CPI conversion factors (Consumer Price Index, 2020 base)
cpi_factors <- tibble(
  year = c(1960, 1970, 1980, 1990, 2000, 2010, 2020),
  cpi_2020 = c(9.21, 6.43, 2.91, 1.87, 1.48, 1.18, 1.00),
  inflation_base = c("1959 nominal", "1970 nominal", "1980 nominal",
                    "1990 nominal", "2000 nominal", "2012 adjusted",
                    "2022 adjusted"),
  cpi_source = "BLS CPI-U, annual average, 2020=1.00"
)

# Function to calculate bracket midpoints for analysis
calculate_bracket_midpoint <- function(income_category) {
  case_when(
    income_category == "<$1,000" ~ 500,
    income_category == "$1,000–1,999" ~ 1500,
    income_category == "$2,000–2,999" ~ 2500,
    income_category == "$3,000–3,999" ~ 3500,
    income_category == "$4,000–4,999" ~ 4500,
    income_category == "$5,000–5,999" ~ 5500,
    income_category == "$6,000–6,999" ~ 6500,
    income_category == "$7,000–7,999" ~ 7500,
    income_category == "$8,000–8,999" ~ 8500,
    income_category == "$9,000–9,999" ~ 9500,
    income_category == "<$10,000" ~ 5000,
    income_category == "$10,000–14,999" ~ 12500,
    income_category == "$15,000–24,999" ~ 20000,
    income_category == "$25,000+" ~ 37500,  # Approximate for distribution analysis
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  )
}

# Apply inflation adjustment
dc_income_with_inflation <- dc_income_all_years %>%
  left_join(cpi_factors, by = "year") %>%
  mutate(
    # Calculate bracket midpoint in original dollars
    bracket_midpoint_original = calculate_bracket_midpoint(income_category),
    
    # Convert to 2020 dollars using CPI
    bracket_midpoint_2020 = bracket_midpoint_original * cpi_2020,
    
    # Academic documentation
    inflation_method = "CPI-U to 2020 dollars",
    adjustment_notes = paste(
      "Original data in", inflation_base, "dollars.",
      "Converted using CPI factor", round(cpi_2020, 2)
    )
  )

cat("✅ Inflation adjustment applied\n")

# ==============================================================================
# 9. CALCULATE INCOME DISTRIBUTION METRICS
# ==============================================================================

cat("\nCalculating income distribution metrics...\n")

dc_income_metrics <- dc_income_with_inflation %>%
  group_by(year, geographic_id, tract_id, location_name) %>%
  summarise(
    total_households = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # Income distribution percentages
    pct_under_10k = sum(count[income_category %in% c("<$1,000", "$1,000–1,999", "$2,000–2,999",
                                                   "$3,000–3,999", "$4,000–4,999", "$5,000–5,999",
                                                   "$6,000–6,999", "$7,000–7,999", "$8,000–8,999",
                                                   "$9,000–9,999", "<$10,000")]) / total_households * 100,
    pct_10k_to_15k = sum(count[income_category == "$10,000–14,999"]) / total_households * 100,
    pct_15k_to_25k = sum(count[income_category == "$15,000–24,999"]) / total_households * 100,
    pct_over_25k = sum(count[income_category == "$25,000+"]) / total_households * 100,
    
    # Income concentration (approximate)
    low_income_share = pct_under_10k + pct_10k_to_15k,
    middle_income_share = pct_15k_to_25k,
    high_income_share = pct_over_25k,
    
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    data_type = "income_distribution_metrics",
    source = "Calculated from income bracket data",
    note = "Percentages of households in each income category. For poverty rates, use official poverty tables."
  )

cat("✅ Income metrics calculated\n")

# ==============================================================================
# 10. SAVE ALL OUTPUT FILES
# ==============================================================================

cat("\nSaving output files...\n")

# 10.1 Save complete income dataset
write_csv(
  dc_income_with_inflation,
  file.path("Cleaned_data_EDJones/dc_income_distributions_1960_2020.csv")
)
cat("dc_income_distributions_1960_2020.csv saved\n")

# 10.2 Save income metrics
write_csv(
  dc_income_metrics,
  file.path("Cleaned_data_EDJones/dc_income_metrics_1960_2020.csv")
) 
cat("Cleaned_data_EDJones/dc_income_metrics_1960_2020.csv saved\n")
View(dc_income_with_inflation)
View(dc_income_metrics)
# 10.3 Create and save metadata
metadata <- list(
  project_info = list(
    project_name = "DC Income Data for Edward P. Jones Literary Analysis",
    institution = "Black Data Lab Internship",
    completion_date = format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d")
  ),
  
  data_sources = tribble(
    ~year, ~source, ~table_code, ~universe, ~dollar_type, ~key_notes,
    1960, "1960 Census", "B8W", "Families", "1959 nominal", "Family income (not households)",
    1970, "NHGIS Time Series", "BS7", "Households", "1970 nominal", "4 income brackets",
    1980, "NHGIS Time Series", "BS7", "Households", "1980 nominal", "4 income brackets",
    1990, "NHGIS Time Series", "BS7", "Households", "1990 nominal", "4 income brackets",
    2000, "NHGIS Time Series", "BS7", "Households", "2000 nominal", "4 income brackets",
    2010, "ACS 2008-2012", "B19001", "Households", "2012 adjusted", "16→4 brackets",
    2020, "ACS 2018-2022", "B19001", "Households", "2022 adjusted", "16→4 brackets"
  ),
  
  inflation_adjustment = list(
    method = "Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U)",
    base_year = 2020,
    source = "Bureau of Labor Statistics, Series CUUR0000SA0",
    factors = cpi_factors,
    purpose = "Enable valid dollar comparisons across decades"
  ),
  
  academic_notes = list(
    unit_caveat = "1960 data uses families while 1970-2020 uses households",
    bracket_mapping = "ACS 16-bracket data mapped to 4 brackets for consistency",
    comparison_recommendation = "Use percentages and inflation-adjusted values for cross-decade analysis",
    poverty_data = "Poverty rates should come from official poverty tables, not estimated from income distributions"
  )
)

# Save metadata as JSON
library(jsonlite)
write_json(
  metadata,
  file.path("Cleaned_data_EDJones/dc_income_metadata.json"),
  pretty = TRUE,
  auto_unbox = TRUE
)
cat("✅ dc_income_metadata.json saved\n")

# 10.4 Create simple README
readme_content <- c(
  "DC INCOME DATA (1960-2020)",
  strrep("=", 60),
  "",
  "FILES:",
  "1. dc_income_distributions_1960_2020.csv - Complete income bracket data",
  "2. dc_income_metrics_1960_2020.csv - Calculated income distribution metrics",
  "3. dc_income_metadata.json - Complete documentation and methodology",
  "",
  "KEY FEATURES:",
  "- Covers 1960, 1970, 1980, 1990, 2000, 2010, 2020",
  "- Income data from Census, NHGIS, and ACS",
  "- Inflation-adjusted to 2020 dollars for comparison",
  "- Standardized 4-category income brackets",
  "",
  "ACADEMIC NOTES:",
  "- 1960: FAMILY income (different unit from later years)",
  "- 1970-2000: HOUSEHOLD income from NHGIS",
  "- 2010-2020: HOUSEHOLD income from ACS",
  "- Use percentages and inflation-adjusted values for comparisons",
  "",
  "CREATED:",
  paste("Date:", format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d")),
  "Project: Black Data Lab Internship - Edward P. Jones Literary Analysis"
)

write_lines(readme_content, file.path("Cleaned_data_EDJones/DCINCOME_README.txt"))
cat("✅ INCOME_README.txt saved\n")


# QUALITY CHECK AND SUMMARY


cat("\n")  
cat(strrep("=", 60))
cat("\n")
cat("PROCESSING COMPLETE: QUALITY SUMMARY\n")
cat("\n")
cat(strrep("=", 60))
cat("\n")

# Data coverage summary
coverage_summary <- dc_income_all_years %>%
  group_by(year, unit) %>%
  summarise(
    tracts = n_distinct(tract_id),
    total_observations = n(),
    total_households = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("DATA COVERAGE SUMMARY:\n")
print(coverage_summary)

# Check for missing data
missing_check <- dc_income_all_years %>%
  summarise(
    total_rows = n(),
    missing_count = sum(is.na(count)),
    zero_count = sum(count == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    missing_pct = round(missing_count / total_rows * 100, 1)
  )

cat("\nDATA QUALITY CHECK:\n")
cat("Total rows:", missing_check$total_rows, "\n")
cat("Missing values:", missing_check$missing_count, "(", missing_check$missing_pct, "%)\n")
cat("Zero values:", missing_check$zero_count, "\n")
```

### Income - Poverty
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)

# Create output directory
output_dir <- "Cleaned_data_EDJones"
if(!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)


# PROCESS 2010 POVERTY DATA (ACS 2008-2012)

cat("Processing 2010 poverty data...\n")
poverty_2010_raw <- read_csv("Data_raw/nhgis0010_ds192_Poverty_lvl_2008_20125_tract.csv") %>%
  clean_names()

dc_poverty_2010_clean <- poverty_2010_raw %>%
  filter(statea == "11") %>%
  select(
    geographic_id = gisjoin,
    tract_id = tracta,
    location_name = name_e,
    poverty_universe = q9qe001,   # Total population for poverty determination
    below_poverty = q9qe002,      # Below poverty level
    above_poverty = q9qe031       # At or above poverty level
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    year = 2010,
    # Guard against division by zero in unpopulated tracts
    poverty_rate = ifelse(poverty_universe > 0, (below_poverty / poverty_universe) * 100, NA_real_),
    source = "ACS 2008-2012 - Poverty Status (B17001 table)",
    note = "5-year estimates. Population for whom poverty status determined.",
    data_type = "poverty_rate",
    geographic_id = as.character(geographic_id)
  )

# PROCESS 2020 POVERTY DATA (ACS 2018-2022)

cat("Processing 2020 poverty data...\n")
poverty_2020_raw <- read_csv("Data_raw/nhgis0010_ds263_Poverty_lvl_2018_20225_tract.csv") %>%
  clean_names()

dc_poverty_2020_clean <- poverty_2020_raw %>%
  filter(statea == "11") %>%
  select(
    geographic_id = gisjoin,
    tract_id = tracta,
    location_name = name_e,
    poverty_universe = aq5ze001,
    below_poverty = aq5ze002,
    above_poverty = aq5ze031
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    year = 2020,
    poverty_rate = ifelse(poverty_universe > 0, (below_poverty / poverty_universe) * 100, NA_real_),
    source = "ACS 2018-2022 - Poverty Status (B17001 table)",
    note = "5-year estimates. Population for whom poverty status determined.",
    data_type = "poverty_rate",
    geographic_id = as.character(geographic_id)
  )


# PROCESS 1970-2000 POVERTY DATA (NHGIS Time Series)

cat("Processing 1970-2000 poverty data...\n")
poverty_raw <- read_csv("Data_raw/nhgis0006_ts_nominal_povertylvl_tract.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  clean_names()

dc_poverty_1970_2000 <- poverty_raw %>%
  filter(state == "District of Columbia") %>%
  select(
    nhgiscode, tracta,
    name1970, name1980, name1990, name2000,
    ax6aa1970, ax6aa1980, ax6aa1990, ax6aa2000, # Universe
    cl6aa1970, cl6aa1980, cl6aa1990, cl6aa2000  # Below
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with(c("ax6", "cl6")),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "population"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    year = str_extract(variable, "\\d{4}") %>% as.integer(),
    measure = case_when(
      str_detect(variable, "ax6aa") ~ "poverty_universe",
      str_detect(variable, "cl6aa") ~ "below_poverty"
    ),
    location_name = case_when(
      year == 1970 ~ name1970,
      year == 1980 ~ name1980,
      year == 1990 ~ name1990,
      year == 2000 ~ name2000
    )
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(id_cols = c(nhgiscode, tracta, location_name, year), 
              names_from = measure, values_from = population) %>%
  mutate(
    geographic_id = as.character(nhgiscode),
    tract_id = as.character(tracta),
    above_poverty = poverty_universe - below_poverty,
    poverty_rate = ifelse(poverty_universe > 0, (below_poverty / poverty_universe) * 100, NA_real_),
    source = "NHGIS Poverty Tables (1970-2000)",
    note = "Decennial census poverty data",
    data_type = "poverty_rate"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(poverty_universe)) %>%
  select(geographic_id, tract_id, location_name, year, poverty_universe, 
         below_poverty, above_poverty, poverty_rate, source, note, data_type)


# COMBINE AND CATEGORIZE

cat("\nCombining all decades...\n")
dc_poverty_all_years <- bind_rows(
  dc_poverty_1970_2000,
  dc_poverty_2010_clean,
  dc_poverty_2020_clean
) %>%
  mutate(
    decade = paste0(floor(year / 10), "0s"),
    poverty_severity = case_when(
      is.na(poverty_rate) ~ "Missing data",
      poverty_rate < 10 ~ "Low poverty (<10%)",
      poverty_rate < 20 ~ "Moderate poverty (10-20%)",
      poverty_rate < 30 ~ "High poverty (20-30%)",
      poverty_rate >= 30 ~ "Very high poverty (30%+)"
    ),
    extreme_poverty_flag = ifelse(poverty_rate >= 40, 1, 0)
  )

# Calculate longitudinal changes within tracts
dc_poverty_final <- dc_poverty_all_years %>%
  group_by(geographic_id) %>%
  arrange(year) %>%
  mutate(
    poverty_rate_change = poverty_rate - lag(poverty_rate),
    pct_relative_change = ((poverty_rate - lag(poverty_rate)) / lag(poverty_rate)) * 100
  ) %>%
  ungroup()


# SAVE OUTPUTS

#write_csv(dc_poverty_final, file.path(output_dir, "dc_poverty_1970_2020_cleaned.csv"))
cat("✅ Saved: dc_poverty_1970_2020_cleaned.csv\n")

# Summary for console validation
summary_stats <- dc_poverty_final %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(mean_rate = mean(poverty_rate, na.rm = TRUE), 
            tract_count = n(),
            .groups = "drop")
print(summary_stats)
View(dc_poverty_final)
```

### Education level
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(jsonlite)

# Create output directory
output_dir <- "Cleaned_data_EDJones"
if(!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)


#  PROCESS 1960 DATA

cat("Processing 1960 Education data...\n")
edu_1960_raw <- read_csv("Data_raw/nhgis0011_ds92_Education1960_tract.csv") %>% clean_names()
dc_edu_1960 <- edu_1960_raw %>% filter(statea == "11")

# Total Attainment (B6B)
dc_edu_1960_total <- dc_edu_1960 %>%
  select(gisjoin, tract_id = tracta, location_name = areaname, b6b001:b6b008) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = b6b001:b6b008, names_to = "variable", values_to = "count") %>%
  mutate(
    attainment_broad = case_when(
      variable %in% c("b6b001", "b6b002", "b6b003", "b6b004", "b6b005") ~ "Less than high school",
      variable == "b6b006" ~ "High school graduate",
      variable == "b6b007" ~ "Some college/Associate's",
      variable == "b6b008" ~ "Bachelor's degree+"
    ),
    year = 1960, population = "total", source = "1960 Census (B6B)"
  ) %>%
  group_by(gisjoin, tract_id, location_name, year, population, attainment_broad, source) %>%
  summarise(count = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# Nonwhite Proxy (B8S)
dc_edu_1960_black <- dc_edu_1960 %>%
  select(gisjoin, tract_id = tracta, location_name = areaname, b8s001:b8s008) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = b8s001:b8s008, names_to = "variable", values_to = "count") %>%
  mutate(
    attainment_broad = case_when(
      variable %in% c("b8s001", "b8s002", "b8s003", "b8s004", "b8s005") ~ "Less than high school",
      variable == "b8s006" ~ "High school graduate",
      variable == "b8s007" ~ "Some college/Associate's",
      variable == "b8s008" ~ "Bachelor's degree+"
    ),
    year = 1960, population = "black_proxy", source = "1960 Census (B8S)"
  ) %>%
  group_by(gisjoin, tract_id, location_name, year, population, attainment_broad, source) %>%
  summarise(count = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")


# PROCESS 1970 DATA

cat("Processing 1970 Education data...\n")
edu_1970_raw <- read_csv("Data_raw/nhgis0011_ds99_Education1970_tract.csv") %>% clean_names()
dc_edu_1970_total <- edu_1970_raw %>%
  filter(statea == "11") %>%
  select(gisjoin, tract_id = tracta, location_name = areaname, c2m001:c2m020) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c2m001:c2m020, names_to = "variable", values_to = "count") %>%
  mutate(
    attainment_broad = case_when(
      variable %in% c("c2m001", "c2m011", "c2m002", "c2m012", "c2m003", "c2m013", 
                       "c2m004", "c2m014", "c2m005", "c2m015", "c2m006", "c2m016") ~ "Less than high school",
      variable %in% c("c2m007", "c2m017") ~ "High school graduate",
      variable %in% c("c2m008", "c2m018") ~ "Some college/Associate's",
      variable %in% c("c2m009", "c2m019", "c2m010", "c2m020") ~ "Bachelor's degree+"
    ),
    year = 1970, population = "total", source = "1970 Census (C2M)"
  ) %>%
  group_by(gisjoin, tract_id, location_name, year, population, attainment_broad, source) %>%
  summarise(count = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")


# PROCESS 1980-2000 DATA (Race-aggregated)


# Process 1980
edu_1980_raw <- read_csv("Data_raw/nhgis0011_ds107_Education1980_tract.csv") %>% clean_names()
dc_edu_1980_total <- edu_1980_raw %>%
  filter(statea == "11") %>%
  select(gisjoin, tract_id = tracta, location_name = areaname, dhn001:dhn025) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = dhn001:dhn025, names_to = "variable", values_to = "count") %>%
  mutate(
    attainment_broad = case_when(
      str_detect(variable, "001|006|011|016|021|002|007|012|017|022") ~ "Less than high school",
      str_detect(variable, "003|008|013|018|023") ~ "High school graduate",
      str_detect(variable, "004|009|014|019|024") ~ "Some college/Associate's",
      str_detect(variable, "005|010|015|020|025") ~ "Bachelor's degree+"
    ),
    year = 1980, population = "total", source = "1980 Census (DHN)"
  ) %>%
  group_by(gisjoin, tract_id, location_name, year, population, attainment_broad, source) %>%
  summarise(count = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

#  PROCESS ACS DATA (2010 & 2020)

cat("Processing ACS 2010 & 2020 Education data...\n")
# 2010 Total
edu_2010_total <- read_csv("Data_raw/nhgis0013_ds191_Education2008_20125_tract.csv") %>%
  clean_names() %>% filter(statea == "11") %>%
  select(gisjoin, tract_id = tracta, location_name = name_e, qure003:qure035) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = qure003:qure035, names_to = "variable", values_to = "count") %>%
  mutate(
    attainment_broad = case_when(
      variable %in% c("qure003", "qure004", "qure005", "qure006", "qure007", "qure008", "qure009", "qure010", 
                       "qure020", "qure021", "qure022", "qure023", "qure024", "qure025", "qure026", "qure027") ~ "Less than high school",
      variable %in% c("qure011", "qure028") ~ "High school graduate",
      variable %in% c("qure012", "qure013", "qure014", "qure029", "qure030", "qure031") ~ "Some college/Associate's",
      variable %in% c("qure015", "qure016", "qure017", "qure018", "qure032", "qure033", "qure034", "qure035") ~ "Bachelor's degree+"
    ),
    year = 2010, population = "total", source = "ACS 2008-2012"
  ) %>%
  group_by(gisjoin, tract_id, location_name, year, population, attainment_broad, source) %>%
  summarise(count = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")


# COMBINE AND STANDARDIZE FIPS

dc_edu_total_all <- bind_rows(
  dc_edu_1960_total, dc_edu_1970_total, dc_edu_1980_total, 
  edu_2010_total # Add your 1990/2000/2020 blocks here following same pattern
) %>%
  mutate(
    geographic_id = as.character(gisjoin),
    tract_id = as.character(tract_id),
    decade = paste0(floor(year / 10), "0s")
  )


#  SAVE OUTPUTS

#write_csv(dc_edu_total_all, file.path(output_dir, "dc_education_total_1960_2020.csv"))
cat("✅ All education data processed and saved.\n")
View(dc_edu_total_all)
```


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)


# PROCESS 1960 MORTALITY (County Level)

# NHGIS 1960 mortality often provides raw counts
mort_1960_raw <- read_csv("Data_raw/nhgis0017_ds230_1960_mortality_county.csv") %>%
  clean_names()

# DC state code in this specific dataset is often "110" or "11"
dc_1960_clean <- mort_1960_raw %>%
  filter(statea == "110" | areaname == "District of Columbia") %>%
  select(total_deaths = agvv001) %>%
  mutate(
    year = 1960,
    population = 763956, # 1960 DC population (Census)
    mortality_rate_per_100k = (total_deaths / population) * 100000,
    source = "NHGIS 1959-1972 Vital Statistics (NT003)",
    data_type = "crude_mortality_rate"
  )


#  PROCESS 1970-2000 MORTALITY (County Level)

# Standardized function to handle the per 10k to per 100k conversion
process_mortality <- function(file_path, year_value) {
  read_csv(file_path, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    clean_names() %>%
    # Use grepl for flexible matching of "District of Columbia"
    filter(str_detect(areaname, "District of Columbia")) %>%
    mutate(
      # Standardizing to per 100k for longitudinal comparison
      mortality_rate_per_100k = agwh001 * 10, 
      year = year_value,
      source = "NHGIS 1970-2007 Vital Statistics (NT005)",
      data_type = "crude_mortality_rate"
    ) %>%
    select(year, mortality_rate_per_100k, source, data_type)
}

# Apply to the 1970-2000 files
dc_1970 <- process_mortality("Data_raw/nhgis0017_ds231_1970_mortality_pertenthousand_county.csv", 1970)
dc_1980 <- process_mortality("Data_raw/nhgis0017_ds231_1980_mortality_county.csv", 1980)
dc_1990 <- process_mortality("Data_raw/nhgis0017_ds231_1990_mortality_county.csv", 1990)
dc_2000 <- process_mortality("Data_raw/nhgis0017_ds231_2000_mortality_county.csv", 2000)


#  COMBINE LONGITUDINAL TREND

dc_mortality_trend <- bind_rows(
  dc_1960_clean %>% select(year, mortality_rate_per_100k, source, data_type),
  dc_1970,
  dc_1980,
  dc_1990,
  dc_2000
) %>%
  arrange(year)

cat("✅ Mortality trend dataset compiled for 1960-2000\n")
print(dc_mortality_trend)
```


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)


# PROCESS CDC WONDER DATA (2010 & 2020)

cat("Processing CDC WONDER mortality data...\n")

# 2010 Data
wonder_2010 <- read_csv("Data_raw/Underlying Cause of Death, 2010.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  clean_names() %>%   
  # Filter for DC and remove footer notes
  filter(!is.na(county), county == "District of Columbia, DC") %>%
  mutate(
    year = 2010,
    total_deaths = deaths,
    # Ensure numeric conversion in case of CDC suppression strings
    mortality_rate_per_100k = as.numeric(crude_rate),
    source = "CDC WONDER - Underlying Cause of Death",
    data_type = "crude_mortality_rate"
  ) %>%
  select(year, total_deaths, population, mortality_rate_per_100k, source, data_type)

# 2020 Data
wonder_2020 <- read_csv("Data_raw/Underlying Cause of Death, 2020-2.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  filter(!is.na(county), county == "District of Columbia, DC") %>%
  mutate(
    year = 2020,
    total_deaths = deaths,
    mortality_rate_per_100k = as.numeric(crude_rate),
    source = "CDC WONDER - Underlying Cause of Death",
    data_type = "crude_mortality_rate"
  ) %>%
  select(year, total_deaths, population, mortality_rate_per_100k, source, data_type)


# STANDARDIZE HISTORICAL DATA (1970-2000)

# Using decennial census counts to provide denominators for historical rates
dc_1970_clean <- dc_1970 %>%
  mutate(total_deaths = NA, population = 756510) %>%
  select(year, total_deaths, population, mortality_rate_per_100k, source, data_type)

dc_1980_clean <- dc_1980 %>%
  mutate(total_deaths = NA, population = 638334) %>%
  select(year, total_deaths, population, mortality_rate_per_100k, source, data_type)

dc_1990_clean <- dc_1990 %>%
  mutate(total_deaths = NA, population = 606900) %>%
  select(year, total_deaths, population, mortality_rate_per_100k, source, data_type)

dc_2000_clean <- dc_2000 %>%
  mutate(total_deaths = NA, population = 572056) %>%
  select(year, total_deaths, population, mortality_rate_per_100k, source, data_type)


# FINAL MERGE AND EXPORT

dc_mortality_all <- bind_rows(
  dc_1960_clean, # From previous mortality block
  dc_1970_clean,
  dc_1980_clean,
  dc_1990_clean,
  dc_2000_clean,
  wonder_2010,
  wonder_2020
) %>%
  arrange(year)

# Save the master mortality file
#write_csv(dc_mortality_all, "Cleaned_data_EDJones/dc_mortality_1960_2020.csv")
cat("✅ Master mortality file saved: Cleaned_data_EDJones/dc_mortality_1960_2020.csv\n")

# Quick check on the trend
print(dc_mortality_all %>% select(year, mortality_rate_per_100k))
View(dc_mortality_all)
```


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)


# LONGITUDINAL CRIME TRENDS (1960-2010)

cat("Processing FBI UCR longitudinal crime data...\n")

# Reading the historical index file
crime_raw <- read_csv(
  "Data_raw/crime rate D.C1960-2019.csv",
  skip = 1, # Skips the metadata header if present
  col_names = c("year", "population", "index")
)

crime_longitudinal_clean <- crime_raw %>%
  filter(!is.na(year)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Clean non-numeric characters (commas, spaces) before converting
    year = as.integer(year),
    population = as.numeric(str_remove_all(as.character(population), ",")),
    index_rate_per_100k = as.numeric(str_remove_all(as.character(index), ","))
  ) %>%
  # Filter for your specific decennial points of interest
  filter(year %in% c(1960, 1970, 1980, 1990, 2000, 2010)) %>%
  mutate(
    source = "FBI UCR Crime Index",
    data_type = "index_crime_rate"
  ) %>%
  select(year, population, index_rate_per_100k, source, data_type)


# SPATIAL CRIME ANALYSIS (2010 Tract Level)

cat("Processing 2010 spatial crime incidents...\n")

crime_2010_raw <- read_csv("Data_raw/Crime_Incidents_in_2010.csv") %>% clean_names()

# Aggregate incidents by census tract
crime_2010_tract <- crime_2010_raw %>%
  filter(!is.na(census_tract)) %>%
  group_by(census_tract) %>%
  summarise(
    total_crime_count = n(),
    violent_crime_count = sum(offense %in% c(
      "HOMICIDE", "ROBBERY", "ASSAULT W/DANGEROUS WEAPON", "SEX ABUSE"
    )),
    property_crime_count = sum(offense %in% c(
      "BURGLARY", "MOTOR VEHICLE THEFT", "THEFT F/AUTO", "THEFT/OTHER", "ARSON"
    )),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    year = 2010,
    # Standardize tract ID format: ensures 6-digit padding (e.g., "000101")
    # This matches the 'tracta' format in your NHGIS education/poverty files
    tract_id = str_pad(as.character(census_tract), 6, pad = "0"),
    geography = "tract",
    source = "DC Open Data Crime Incidents",
    data_type = "crime_counts_by_tract"
  )


# SAVE AND VALIDATE

#write_csv(crime_longitudinal_clean, "Cleaned_data_EDJones/dc_crime_longitudinal_1960_2010.csv")
#write_csv(crime_2010_tract, "Cleaned_data_EDJones/dc_crime_tract_2010.csv")

cat("✅ Crime datasets saved.\n")
cat("Summary of 2010 Tracts:", nrow(crime_2010_tract), "tracts identified.\n")

View(crime_2010_tract)

# PROCESS 2020 SPATIAL CRIME (New Block)
cat("Processing 2020 spatial crime incidents...\n")

# Make sure this filename matches your actual 2020 CSV file in Data_raw
crime_2020_raw <- read_csv("Data_raw/Crime_Incidents_in_2020.csv") %>% clean_names()

crime_2020_tract <- crime_2020_raw %>%
  filter(!is.na(census_tract)) %>%
  group_by(census_tract) %>%
  summarise(
    total_crime = n(),  # Named 'total_crime' to match your city summary logic
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    year = 2020,
    tract_id = str_pad(as.character(census_tract), 6, pad = "0"),
    geography = "tract"
  )
```


```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# 1. Standardize the 2010 City Summary
crime_2010_city <- crime_2010_tract %>%
  summarise(total_crime = sum(total_crime_count, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(
    year = 2010,
    geography = "city",
    population = 601723, # 2010 Census official count for DC
    crime_rate_per_100k = (total_crime / population) * 100000,
    source = "DC Open Data Crime Incidents"
  )

# 2. Standardize the 2020 City Summary
crime_2020_city <- crime_2020_tract %>%
  summarise(total_crime = sum(total_crime, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% # Assumes 2020 uses 'total_crime'
  mutate(
    year = 2020,
    geography = "city",
    population = 689545, # 2020 Census official count for DC
    crime_rate_per_100k = (total_crime / population) * 100000,
    source = "DC Open Data Crime Incidents"
  )

# 3. Clean up the historical 'crime_clean' dataframe

crime_historical_city <- crime_longitudinal_clean %>%
 # If index_rate_per_100k exists, rename it. If not, move on.
 rename(any_of(c(crime_rate_per_100k = "index_rate_per_100k"))) %>%
 mutate(
 geography = "city",
 # Ensure total_crime exists as a column for the bind_rows later
total_crime = if("total_crime" %in% names(.)) total_crime else NA_real_
  ) %>%
 # Ensure all necessary columns are present and numeric where needed
  select(year, population, total_crime, crime_rate_per_100k, source, geography)

# 4. Final Bind
dc_crime_citywide <- bind_rows(
  crime_historical_city,
  crime_2010_city,
  crime_2020_city
) %>%
  # Keep the UCR/Historical 2010 record for longitudinal consistency
  filter(!(year == 2010 & source == "DC Open Data Crime Incidents")) %>%
  arrange(year)

# Preview to ensure it worked
print(tail(dc_crime_citywide))

#write_csv(dc_crime_citywide, "Cleaned_data_EDJones/dc_crime_citywide_1960_2020.csv")
```


```{r}
# 1. Ensure column names match between 2010 and 2020 tract data
crime_2010_tract_renamed <- crime_2010_tract %>%
  rename(
    total_crime = total_crime_count,
    violent_crime = violent_crime_count,
    property_crime = property_crime_count
  )

# 2. Combine the datasets
dc_crime_tract <- bind_rows(
  crime_2010_tract_renamed,
  crime_2020_tract
) %>%

  # If you must re-pad, use the lowercase 'census_tract'
  mutate(tract_id = str_pad(as.character(tract_id), 6, side = "left", pad = "0"))

# 3. Save
write_csv(dc_crime_tract, "Cleaned_data_EDJones/dc_crime_tract_2010_2020.csv")
cat("✅ City-wide and Tract-level crime datasets harmonized and saved.\n")
```

### Geographic ID & FIPS Standardization
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)

output_dir <- "Cleaned_data_EDJones"
# Create a 'Final' subdirectory if it doesn't exist
final_dir <- file.path(output_dir, "Final")
if (!dir.exists(final_dir)) dir.create(final_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# 1. HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
pad_tract <- function(x) {
  # Handles numeric, character, and scientific notation
  clean <- format(x, scientific = FALSE, trim = TRUE)
  clean <- str_replace(clean, "\\.0$", "") 
  # Pad to 6 digits to match NHGIS 'tracta' standards
  str_pad(clean, width = 6, side = "left", pad = "0")
}

# 2. STANDARDIZATION PIPELINE
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
process_standard_file <- function(filename) {
  df <- read_csv(file.path(output_dir, filename), show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    clean_names()
  
  # Check if we need to rename standard NHGIS columns
  if ("nhgiscode" %in% names(df)) df <- rename(df, geographic_id = nhgiscode)
  if ("tracta" %in% names(df)) df <- rename(df, tract_id = tracta)
  if ("tract_name" %in% names(df)) df <- rename(df, location_name = tract_name)
  
  # Create the join ID
  # If 'tract_id' isn't there, we check for 'census_tract' (common in Crime/CDC files)
  target_col <- intersect(names(df), c("tract_id", "census_tract"))[1]
  
  df %>%
    mutate(tract_join_id = pad_tract(!!sym(target_col))) %>%
    write_csv(file.path(final_dir, str_replace(filename, ".csv", "_standard.csv")))
}

# List of files to process through the pipeline
files_to_process <- c(
  "dc_black_population_percentage_1960_2020.csv",
  "dc_black_population_change_1960_2020.csv",
  "dc_black_segregation_categories.csv",
  "dc_age_sex_total_1960_2020.csv",
  "dc_income_distributions_1960_2020.csv",
  "dc_income_metrics_1960_2020.csv",
  "dc_education_total_1960_2020.csv",
  "dc_crime_tract_2010_2020.csv"
)

# Execute processing
walk(files_to_process, ~try(process_standard_file(.x)))

# 3. FIPS LOCKDOWN (11-Digit Standard)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n--- Locking FIPS codes as 11-digit strings ---\n")

standard_files <- list.files(final_dir, pattern = "_standard.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

for (fpath in standard_files) {
  # Read as character to prevent R from turning IDs into numbers/scientific notation
  df <- read_csv(fpath, col_types = cols(.default = "c")) %>%
    mutate(
      # DC FIPS = 11 (State) + 001 (County) + 6-digit Tract
      fips_full = paste0("11001", tract_join_id)
    )
  
  write_csv(df, fpath)
  cat("Locked FIPS in:", basename(fpath), "\n")
}

# 4. VERIFICATION
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n--- VERIFICATION: FIPS Samples ---\n")
check_df <- read_csv(standard_files[1], col_types = cols(.default = "c"))
print(check_df %>% select(year, tract_join_id, fips_full) %>% head(3))
```


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(tigris)

final_dir <- "Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final"

# --- PART A: The Fixed Zero-Population Function ---
fix_empty_tracts <- function(df) {
  # 1. Identify target columns (percents/shares/etc.)
  target_cols <- names(df)[str_detect(names(df), "percent|share|pct_")]
  
  # 2. If no target columns or no denominators, just return the df
  if (length(target_cols) == 0) return(df)
  
  # 3. Apply fix based on which denominator exists in THIS file
  if ("total_population" %in% names(df)) {
    df <- df %>%
      mutate(across(any_of(target_cols), 
                    ~ if_else(is.na(.x) & total_population == 0, 0, .x)))
  }
  
  if ("total_households" %in% names(df)) {
    df <- df %>%
      mutate(across(any_of(target_cols), 
                    ~ if_else(is.na(.x) & total_households == 0, 0, .x)))
  }
  
  return(df)
}

# --- PART B: Execution Loop ---
files_to_clean <- list.files(final_dir, pattern = "_standard.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

for (f in files_to_clean) {
  # Read as character, convert types for math, apply the targeted fix, then save
  df_cleaned <- read_csv(f, col_types = cols(.default = "c"), show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    type_convert(guess_integer = FALSE) %>%
    fix_empty_tracts() %>%
    mutate(
      tract_join_id = str_pad(as.character(tract_join_id), 6, "left", "0"),
      fips_full = paste0("11001", tract_join_id)
    )
  
  write_csv(df_cleaned, f)
  cat("Successfully updated and FIPS-locked:", basename(f), "\n")
}

# --- PART C: Verification ---
dc_geo <- tracts(state = "11", cb = TRUE, year = 2020) %>%
  mutate(fips_full = as.character(GEOID))

test_income <- read_csv(file.path(final_dir, "dc_income_metrics_1960_2020_standard.csv"), 
                        col_types = cols(fips_full = "c"))

merged_test <- dc_geo %>% left_join(test_income, by = "fips_full")

cat("\n--- FINAL VERIFICATION ---\n")
cat("Geometry Join Success:", sum(!is.na(merged_test$total_households)), "tracts matched.\n")
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(tigris)
options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)

output_dir <- "Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final"
shape_out <- file.path(output_dir, "shapefiles")
if (!dir.exists(shape_out)) dir.create(shape_out)

# 1. Get the authoritative 2020 D.C. Geometry
dc_geo <- tracts(state = "11", cb = TRUE, year = 2020) %>%
  select(fips_full = GEOID) %>%
  mutate(fips_full = as.character(fips_full))

# 2. Files to process
geo_files <- c(
  "dc_black_population_change_1960_2020_standard.csv",
  "dc_black_population_percentage_1960_2020_standard.csv",
  "dc_black_segregation_categories_standard.csv",
  "dc_crime_tract_2010_2020_standard.csv",
  "dc_education_total_1960_2020_standard.csv",
  "dc_income_metrics_1960_2020_standard.csv"
)

for (fname in geo_files) {
  fpath <- file.path(output_dir, fname)
  
  # Read and IMPROVE ID matching
  df <- read_csv(fpath, col_types = cols(.default = "c")) %>%
    mutate(
      # We clean the ID but keep the original 'year' and data intact
      clean_tract = str_replace_all(tract_join_id, "\\.", ""),
      tract_6 = str_pad(clean_tract, 6, side = "left", pad = "0"),
      fips_full = paste0("11001", tract_6)
    )
  
  # 3. Join with Geometry
  # CHANGE: We use right_join or keep the data-side primary to ensure 1960 survives
  # even if it has a 'null' geometry.
  df_sf <- dc_geo %>% 
    right_join(df, by = "fips_full") 

  # 4. REMOVED: filter(!is.na(year)) 
  # This filter was the reason 1960 was disappearing. 
  # By removing it, the 1960 rows stay in the file for Tableau.
  
  # 5. Save as GeoJSON
  # GeoJSON handles NA geometries better than Shapefiles
  st_write(df_sf, file.path(output_dir, str_replace(fname, ".csv", ".geojson")), 
           delete_dsn = TRUE, quiet = TRUE)
  
  # 6. Save as Shapefile
  # Note: Rows with NO geometry will still be in the .DBF (spreadsheet) 
  # part of the shapefile, which allows the Tableau filter to see them.
  st_write(df_sf, file.path(shape_out, str_replace(fname, ".csv", ".shp")), 
           delete_dsn = TRUE, quiet = TRUE)
  
  cat(sprintf("✅ Fixed %s: All years (including 1960) preserved.\n", fname))
}

```


```{r}
# Install if needed: install.packages("zip")
library(tidyverse)
library(zip) 

output_dir <- "Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final"
zip_dir <- file.path(output_dir, "Zipped_Exports")
if (!dir.exists(zip_dir)) dir.create(zip_dir)

# 1. ZIP THE CSV FILES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
csv_files <- list.files(output_dir, pattern = "_standard\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

for (f in csv_files) {
  zip_name <- str_replace(basename(f), "\\.csv$", ".zip")
  zip_path <- file.path(zip_dir, zip_name)
  
  # zip::zipr is better at handling relative paths and individual files
  zip::zipr(zip_path, files = f)
  cat("Created ZIP for CSV:", zip_name, "\n")
}

# 2. ZIP THE SHAPEFILES (Bundling all components: .shp, .dbf, .prj, etc.)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
shape_dir <- file.path(output_dir, "shapefiles")
# Find unique base names (e.g., "dc_income_metrics_1960_2020_standard")
shp_bases <- list.files(shape_dir, pattern = "\\.shp$") %>% str_remove("\\.shp$")

for (base in shp_bases) {
  # Collect all 4-6 files that make up this specific shapefile
  all_components <- list.files(shape_dir, pattern = paste0("^", base), full.names = TRUE)
  
  zip_name <- paste0(base, "_shapefile.zip")
  zip_path <- file.path(zip_dir, zip_name)
  
  # Bundle them all into one ZIP
  zip::zipr(zip_path, files = all_components)
  cat("Created Bundle for Shapefile:", zip_name, "\n")
}

cat("\n--- DONE: Individual ZIP files created in /Zipped_Exports ---")
```


```{r}
library(sf)
library(tidyverse)
library(tigris)
options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)

# Reload 1960 and 1970 NHGIS boundaries
geo_1960_raw <- st_read("Data_raw/nhgis_1960/US_tract_1960_conflated.shp", quiet = TRUE)
geo_1970_raw <- st_read("Data_raw/nhgis_1970/US_tract_1970_conflated.shp", quiet = TRUE)

# Filter to DC
dc_1960 <- geo_1960_raw %>% filter(NHGISST == "110")
dc_1970 <- geo_1970_raw %>% filter(NHGISST == "110")

cat("DC 1960 tracts:", nrow(dc_1960), "\n")
cat("DC 1970 tracts:", nrow(dc_1970), "\n")
```



```{r}
# Build all geometry sources
geo_1960 <- dc_1960 %>% select(GISJOIN, geometry) %>% st_transform(4326)

geo_1970 <- dc_1970 %>%
  mutate(GISJOIN = if_else(
    nchar(GISJOIN) == 12,
    paste0("G1100010", str_pad(as.integer(str_sub(GISJOIN, 9, 12)), 4, pad = "0"), "00"),
    GISJOIN)) %>%
  select(GISJOIN, geometry) %>% st_transform(4326)

geo_1990 <- tracts(state = "DC", year = 1990, cb = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(fips_full = paste0(STATEFP, COUNTYFP, TRACTBASE, TRACTSUF)) %>%
  select(fips_full, geometry) %>% st_transform(4326)

geo_2000 <- tracts(state = "DC", year = 2000, cb = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(fips_full = paste0(STATEFP, COUNTYFP, TRACT)) %>%
  select(fips_full, geometry) %>% st_transform(4326)

geo_2010 <- tracts(state = "DC", year = 2010) %>%
  mutate(fips_full = paste0(STATEFP10, COUNTYFP10, TRACTCE10)) %>%
  select(fips_full, geometry) %>% st_transform(4326)

geo_2020 <- tracts(state = "DC", year = 2020) %>%
  mutate(fips_full = paste0(STATEFP, COUNTYFP, TRACTCE)) %>%
  select(fips_full, geometry) %>% st_transform(4326)

# Read income CSV
income_df <- read_csv(
  "Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final/dc_income_metrics_1960_2020_standard.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE,
  col_types = cols(fips_full = col_character())
)

# Build year-matched spatial file
years <- unique(income_df$year)

income_geo <- map_dfr(years, function(yr) {
  if (yr == 1960) {
    income_df %>%
      filter(year == yr) %>%
      left_join(geo_1960, by = c("geographic_id" = "GISJOIN")) %>%
      st_as_sf()
  } else if (yr == 1970) {
    income_df %>%
      filter(year == yr) %>%
      left_join(geo_1970, by = c("geographic_id" = "GISJOIN")) %>%
      st_as_sf()
  } else {
    geo <- switch(as.character(yr),
      "1980" = geo_1990,
      "1990" = geo_1990,
      "2000" = geo_2000,
      "2010" = geo_2010,
      geo_2020)
    income_df %>%
      filter(year == yr) %>%
      left_join(geo, by = "fips_full") %>%
      st_as_sf()
  }
})

# Verify match counts per year
income_geo %>%
  mutate(has_polygon = !st_is_empty(geometry)) %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(with_polygon = sum(has_polygon), without_polygon = sum(!has_polygon))

st_write(
  income_geo,
  "Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final/shapefiles/dc_income_metrics_1960_2020_standard.shp",
  delete_dsn = TRUE,
  quiet = TRUE
)

cat("✅ Income metrics shapefile rebuilt with correct year-matched geometry\n")
```


```{r}
# 1. Define the full path to your shapefile (without extension)
shp_path <- "./Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final/shapefiles/dc_income_metrics_1960_2020_standard"

# 2. List all component files (add .cpg or .xml if they exist)
shp_files <- paste0(shp_path, c(".shp", ".shx", ".dbf", ".prj"))

# 3. Check which files actually exist (safety check)
existing_files <- shp_files[file.exists(shp_files)]
if(length(existing_files) < 4) {
  warning("Missing some shapefile components. Found: ", paste(basename(existing_files), collapse=", "))
}

# 4. Create the ZIP archive
zip_file <- paste0(shp_path, ".zip")
utils::zip(zipfile = zip_file, files = existing_files, flags = "-r9X")

# 5. Confirm success
cat("✅ ZIP created at:", zip_file, "\n")
cat("Size:", file.size(zip_file) / 1024, "KB\n")
```

```{r}
library(readxl)

excel_sheets("Data_raw/Lost in the City - Complete Dataset - 2-4-25.xlsx")
```


```{r}
library(readxl)

ej_locations <- read_excel(
  "Data_raw/Lost in the City - Complete Dataset - 2-4-25.xlsx",
  sheet = "Geography-Settings"
)

names(ej_locations)
nrow(ej_locations)
head(ej_locations, 3)
```


```{r}
library(readxl)
library(sf)
library(tidyverse)
library(tigris)
options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)

# Read the locations
ej_locations <- read_excel(
  "Data_raw/Lost in the City - Complete Dataset - 2-4-25.xlsx",
  sheet = "Geography-Settings"
) %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  filter(!is.na(latitude) & !is.na(longitude)) %>%
  mutate(
    latitude = as.numeric(latitude),
    longitude = as.numeric(longitude)
  )

cat("EJ locations with coordinates:", nrow(ej_locations), "\n")

# Convert to spatial points
ej_sf <- ej_locations %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326)

# Get 2020 DC tract boundaries
dc_tracts_2020 <- tracts(state = "DC", year = 2020) %>%
  mutate(fips_full = paste0(STATEFP, COUNTYFP, TRACTCE)) %>%
  select(fips_full, geometry) %>%
  st_transform(4326)

# Spatial join - assign each location to its census tract
ej_with_tract <- st_join(ej_sf, dc_tracts_2020, join = st_within)

cat("Locations matched to tracts:", sum(!is.na(ej_with_tract$fips_full)), "\n")
cat("Locations outside DC tracts:", sum(is.na(ej_with_tract$fips_full)), "\n")

# Summarise by tract - concatenate all story/location info per tract
ej_by_tract <- ej_with_tract %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  filter(!is.na(fips_full)) %>%
  group_by(fips_full) %>%
  summarise(
    ej_location_count = n(),
    ej_story_titles = paste(unique(story_title), collapse = " | "),
    ej_location_names = paste(unique(location_name), collapse = " | "),
    ej_space_types = paste(unique(space_type), collapse = " | "),
    ej_setting_types = paste(unique(setting_type), collapse = " | "),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("Tracts with EJ locations:", nrow(ej_by_tract), "\n")
print(head(ej_by_tract, 3))
```


```{r}
# Join EJ data to all existing shapefiles and re-export
shapefile_dir <- "Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final/shapefiles"
shp_files <- list.files(shapefile_dir, pattern = "\\.shp$", full.names = TRUE)

for (fpath in shp_files) {
  fname <- basename(fpath)
  
  shp <- st_read(fpath, quiet = TRUE)
  
  # Check which FIPS column exists in this shapefile
  fips_col <- intersect(names(shp), c("fps_fll", "fips_full"))[1]
  
  if (is.na(fips_col)) {
    cat(sprintf("⚠️ Skipped (no FIPS column): %s\n", fname))
    next
  }
  
  shp_with_ej <- shp %>%
    left_join(ej_by_tract, by = setNames("fips_full", fips_col)) %>%
    mutate(
      ej_loc_cnt = replace_na(ej_location_count, 0),
      ej_stories = replace_na(ej_story_titles, ""),
      ej_locatns = replace_na(ej_location_names, ""),
      ej_spaces  = replace_na(ej_space_types, ""),
      ej_setting = replace_na(ej_setting_types, "")
    ) %>%
    select(-any_of(c("ej_location_count", "ej_story_titles", 
                     "ej_location_names", "ej_space_types", "ej_setting_types")))
  
  st_write(shp_with_ej, fpath, delete_dsn = TRUE, quiet = TRUE)
  cat(sprintf("✅ Updated: %s\n", fname))
}

# Save EJ locations as standalone point shapefile
ej_sf_export <- ej_with_tract %>%
  select(
    story_title,
    location_name,
    space_type,
    setting_type,
    interior_or_exterior,
    demographic,
    address,
    fips_full,
    notes
  )

st_write(
  ej_sf_export,
  file.path(shapefile_dir, "edward_jones_locations.shp"),
  delete_dsn = TRUE,
  quiet = TRUE
)

cat("\n✅ Edward Jones standalone point shapefile saved\n")
cat("Tracts with EJ locations:", nrow(ej_by_tract), "\n")
cat("Total EJ location points:", nrow(ej_sf_export), "\n")
```


```{r}
library(zip)

# Define shapefile name (without extension)
shp_name <- "edward_jones_locations"
shp_dir  <- "Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final/shapefiles"

# List all files that belong to the shapefile
shp_files <- list.files(
  shp_dir,
  pattern = paste0("^", shp_name, "\\."),
  full.names = TRUE
)

# Define zip output path
zip_path <- file.path(shp_dir, "Edward_jones_location.zip")

# Create zip file
zip::zip(
  zipfile = zip_path,
  files = shp_files
)

cat("✅ Edward Jones shapefile zipped for ArcGIS\n")
cat("📁 Location:", zip_path, "\n")
```

df <- st_read("Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final/shapefiles/dc_black_population_change_1960_2020_standard.shp")
names(df)  
```

df <- st_read("Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final/shapefiles/dc_black_population_percentage_1960_2020_standard.shp")
names(df)  
```

df <- st_read("Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final/shapefiles/dc_black_segregation_categories_standard.shp")
names(df)  
```

names(df)

names(df)

names(df)

library(sf)
df <- st_read("Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final/shapefiles/dc_mortality_1960_2020.shp")
names(df)
```



```{r}
library(sf)
library(tidyverse)

df <- st_read("Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final/shapefiles/dc_income_metrics_1960_2020_standard.shp")

# Restore full column names
df <- df %>%
  rename(
    geographic_id     = ggrphc_,
    tract_id          = tract_d,
    location_name     = lctn_nm,
    total_households  = ttl_hsh,
    pct_under_10k     = pct__10,
    pct_10k_to_15k    = p_10__1,
    pct_15k_to_25k    = p_15__2,
    pct_over_25k      = pct__25,
    low_income_share  = lw_ncm_,
    middle_income_share = mddl_n_,
    high_income_share = hgh_nc_,
    data_type         = dat_typ,
    tract_join_id     = trct_j_,
    fips_full         = fps_fll
  )

# GeoJSON for Flourish maps
dir.create("flourish_exports", showWarnings = FALSE)
st_write(df, "flourish_exports/dc_income_metrics.geojson", delete_dsn = TRUE)

# CSV for Flourish charts
df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  write_csv("flourish_exports/dc_income_metrics.csv")
```

```{r}

#BLACK POPULATION CHANGE 
st_read("Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final/shapefiles/dc_black_population_change_1960_2020_standard.shp", quiet = TRUE) %>%
  rename(
    fips_full            = fps_fll,
    geographic_id        = ggrphc_,
    tract_id             = tract_d,
    location_name        = lctn_nm,
    black_population     = blck_pp,
    total_population     = ttl_ppl,
    black_percent        = blck_pr,
    black_pop_change     = blck_p_,
    black_pop_pct_change = blck___,
    tract_join_id        = trct_j_,
    clean_tract          = cln_trc
  ) %>% {
    st_write(., "flourish_exports/dc_black_population_change.geojson", delete_dsn = TRUE, quiet = TRUE)
    st_drop_geometry(.) %>% write_csv("flourish_exports/dc_black_population_change.csv")
    cat("✅ black_population_change done\n")
  }

# BLACK POPULATION PERCENTAGE
st_read("Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final/shapefiles/dc_black_population_percentage_1960_2020_standard.shp", quiet = TRUE) %>%
  rename(
    fips_full        = fps_fll,
    geographic_id    = ggrphc_,
    tract_id         = tract_d,
    location_name    = lctn_nm,
    black_population = blck_pp,
    total_population = ttl_ppl,
    black_percent    = blck_pr,
    tract_join_id    = trct_j_,
    clean_tract      = cln_trc
  ) %>% {
    st_write(., "flourish_exports/dc_black_population_percentage.geojson", delete_dsn = TRUE, quiet = TRUE)
    st_drop_geometry(.) %>% write_csv("flourish_exports/dc_black_population_percentage.csv")
    cat("✅ black_population_percentage done\n")
  }

# BLACK SEGREGATION CATEGORIES
st_read("Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final/shapefiles/dc_black_segregation_categories_standard.shp", quiet = TRUE) %>%
  rename(
    fips_full            = fps_fll,
    geographic_id        = ggrphc_,
    tract_id             = tract_d,
    location_name        = lctn_nm,
    black_population     = blck_pp,
    total_population     = ttl_ppl,
    black_percent        = blck_pr,
    segregation_category = sgrgtn_,
    tract_join_id        = trct_j_,
    clean_tract          = cln_trc
  ) %>% {
    st_write(., "flourish_exports/dc_black_segregation_categories.geojson", delete_dsn = TRUE, quiet = TRUE)
    st_drop_geometry(.) %>% write_csv("flourish_exports/dc_black_segregation_categories.csv")
    cat("✅ black_segregation_categories done\n")
  }

# CRIME TRACT
st_read("Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final/shapefiles/dc_crime_tract_2010_2020_standard.shp", quiet = TRUE) %>%
  rename(
    fips_full      = fps_fll,
    census_tract   = cnss_tr,
    total_crime    = ttl_crm,
    violent_crime  = vlnt_cr,
    property_crime = prprty_,
    tract_id       = tract_d,
    geography      = gegrphy,
    data_type      = dat_typ,
    tract_join_id  = trct_j_,
    clean_tract    = cln_trc
  ) %>% {
    st_write(., "flourish_exports/dc_crime_tract.geojson", delete_dsn = TRUE, quiet = TRUE)
    st_drop_geometry(.) %>% write_csv("flourish_exports/dc_crime_tract.csv")
    cat("✅ crime_tract done\n")
  }

# CRIME CITYWIDE
st_read("Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final/shapefiles/dc_crime_citywide_1960_2020.shp", quiet = TRUE) %>%
  rename(crime_rate_per_100k = crime_rate) %>% {
    st_write(., "flourish_exports/dc_crime_citywide.geojson", delete_dsn = TRUE, quiet = TRUE)
    st_drop_geometry(.) %>% write_csv("flourish_exports/dc_crime_citywide.csv")
    cat("✅ crime_citywide done\n")
  }

# EDUCATION
st_read("Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final/shapefiles/dc_education_total_1960_2020_standard.shp", quiet = TRUE) %>%
  rename(
    fips_full        = fps_fll,
    tract_id         = tract_d,
    location_name    = lctn_nm,
    population       = popultn,
    attainment_broad = attnmn_,
    geographic_id    = ggrphc_,
    tract_join_id    = trct_j_,
    clean_tract      = cln_trc
  ) %>% {
    st_write(., "flourish_exports/dc_education_total.geojson", delete_dsn = TRUE, quiet = TRUE)
    st_drop_geometry(.) %>% write_csv("flourish_exports/dc_education_total.csv")
    cat("✅ education_total done\n")
  }

# MORTALITY 
st_read("Cleaned_data_EDJones/Final/shapefiles/dc_mortality_1960_2020.shp", quiet = TRUE) %>%
  rename(
    total_deaths          = total_deat,
    mortality_rate_per_100k = mortality_
  ) %>% {
    st_write(., "flourish_exports/dc_mortality.geojson", delete_dsn = TRUE, quiet = TRUE)
    st_drop_geometry(.) %>% write_csv("flourish_exports/dc_mortality.csv")
    cat("✅ mortality done\n")
  }

# INCOME METRICS 
cat("\nAll exports complete. Files in flourish_exports/:\n")
list.files("flourish_exports")
```
